titel: Penalties for violations of the EU's new regulations concerning fluorinated gases (F-gases) and ozone-depleting substances.
html:
The proposal suggests changes to the environmental code's criminal provisions, aiming to complement the EU's new regulations on fluorinated greenhouse gases (F-gases) and ozone-depleting substances (ODS).

It is proposed that the criminal provisions regarding the handling of hazardous chemicals be adapted to the new regulations. Furthermore, it is proposed that violations of additional prohibitions and restrictions under the new regulations be subject to penalties. These changes include making it punishable to release fluorinated greenhouse gases and ozone-depleting substances into the atmosphere. Additionally, the proposed criminal liability would extend to additional stages in the handling of such gases. It is also proposed that violations of the documentation requirements when using sulfuryl fluoride for fumigation be punishable by fines.

Most of the proposed amendments to the law are scheduled to come into effect on January 1, 2026. The amendments concerning the prohibitions and restrictions on the use of fluorinated greenhouse gases for maintenance and servicing of stationary cooling equipment and electrical circuit breakers are proposed to come into effect on January 1, 2032, and January 1, 2035, respectively.

Proposed Parliamentary Decisions

The government's proposal:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the environmental code.
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the environmental code.
  3. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the environmental code.

4.
tldr: The government intends to introduce stricter penalties to protect the environment from harmful gases. It will become illegal to release greenhouse gases and substances that damage the ozone layer in an improper manner.
lattlast:
The government has proposed changes to the Environmental Code, the law that governs how we protect nature. The purpose is to comply with new EU regulations regarding so-called fluorinated gases (F-gases) and ozone-depleting substances. These gases are dangerous because they contribute to climate change and damage the ozone layer surrounding the Earth.

Violations of the new regulations should result in penalties. For example, it is proposed that it become punishable to release these gases into the air. The handling of these gases will also be more strictly controlled to prevent environmental damage. Failure to provide the correct documentation when using the gas sulfuryl fluoride may result in fines.

Most of the new regulations are proposed to come into effect on January 1, 2026. Certain parts of the proposal involve prohibiting the use of specific gases in the servicing of refrigeration equipment and electrical equipment. These specific prohibitions will take effect later, on January 1, 2032, and January 1, 2035, respectively.
